title: Chamber of Guf split
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jan 06, 2012 9:31 am symbv wrote: As I said earlier, Ritsuko was talking mainly about Eva when she talked about Chamber of Guf there. There are two Chambers of Guf, one from Lilith and one from Adam, but most probably Ritsuko is talking about only one Chamber.... And we know all the production EVAs (and most of all EVAs) were derived from Adam whose Chamber of Guf was empty after 2I. It is likely that when Ritsuko was saying EVA she was talking about EVA as made in the Adam Plan as production type -- in Ep.21 Naoko also said EVA was what human artificially created from Adam. (also I wonder whether Ritsuko was involved when EVA00 and EVA01 were built)

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Fri Jan 06, 2012 9:36 am Bagheera wrote: Isn't it significant that she was talking about this in the context of Rei, the Dummy System, and Unit 01, all derived from Lilith?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jan 06, 2012 9:40 am Bagheera wrote: Isn't it significant that she was talking about this in the context of Rei, the Dummy System, and Unit 01, all derived from Lilith? I've always thought Ritsuko's monologue indicated Lilith's chamber was empty, that Rei was born with a soul (Lilith's soul), and that all the others (the Evas) needed harvested souls. She was talking about Eva in general there, but in the context of explaining what Rei is. Referring to Adam in that context doesn't make sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jan 06, 2012 10:06 am Reichu wrote: I don't disagree that your proposal makes sense. I suppose if there is any reason for resistance, there is the fact that if Ritsuko thinks Rei is made from Adam, then what she says is irredeemable gibberish, and not merely gibberish that we haven't "interpreted" enough.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jan 06, 2012 10:08 am Monk Ed wrote: ...Unless she thinks Lilith is actually Adam (or at least doesn't know that Eva-01 or Rei were made from Lilith).

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jan 06, 2012 10:12 am Bagheera wrote: That's a good point. If that's the case she can't be talking about Adam's Chamber of Guf.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jan 06, 2012 10:16 am Monk Ed wrote: Irredeemable how? As in there's nothing more to be done with it because she was going off of wrong information?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jan 06, 2012 10:40 am Reichu wrote: Things get messy rather quickly, don't they? The first question raised in my mind is, if Ritsuko is talking about Adam's Chamber of Guf, how does THAT explain why Rei has a soul and the Evas don't? Next is, are we supposed to retroactively ignore what she says about Evas being born soulless in the case of Eva-01?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jan 06, 2012 10:45 am Reichu wrote: The first question raised in my mind is, if Ritsuko is talking about Adam's Chamber of Guf, how does THAT explain why Rei has a soul and the Evas don't?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Fri Jan 06, 2012 2:03 pm Monk Ed wrote: ...Unless she thinks Lilith is actually Adam (or at least doesn't know that Eva-01 or Rei were made from Lilith).

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jan 06, 2012 2:12 pm symbv wrote: It is possible. This is why I asked if Ritsuko's entry into service came after EVA00 and EV01 were built...

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Fri Jan 06, 2012 2:17 pm ^ And this fits perfectly with the "Japanese Consensus Interpretation" that Ritsuko was talking about EVAs and Adam's Chamber of Guf being empty. We don't even need to theorize why she seems to refer only to the production type (which was built from Adam). She might have actually meant all EVAs when she said they did not have soul as (Adam's) Chamber of Guf was empty.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 06, 2012 8:19 pm However, Ritsuko is clearly in the loop of knowing that Unit-01 is different than the other Evangelions. If she doesn't think that Unit-01 was derived from Lilith (due to her having no knowledge of Lilith) than what could she possibly think made Unit-01 special?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jan 06, 2012 8:26 pm Sachi wrote: However, Ritsuko is clearly in the loop of knowing that Unit-01 is different than the other Evangelions. If she doesn't think that Unit-01 was derived from Lilith (due to her having no knowledge of Lilith) than what could she possibly think made Unit-01 special?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 06, 2012 9:00 pm Monk Ed wrote: She might never have questioned it, the same way nobody else did even though the commander clearly favored Unit 01 over the others. It's often amazing how far people will go without even asking questions so long as someone with sufficiently projected authority is the one telling them to do things (as the Milgram experiments indicate). This actually goes double for Japanese society, where complete obedience to authority is pretty much the norm.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jan 06, 2012 9:07 pm Sachi wrote: I highly doubt this, considering Ritsuko's character. She seems to exhibit a fundamental understanding of what makes Unit-01 special, even though we never explicitly hear it from her lips what those special details are. She is much more knowledgeable of the inner-workings of the Evangelion than most everybody in the series. Claiming she's ignorant of this detail throws away all the subtleties of her character throughout the entire series, and the conflict between herself and Misato over Unit-01.
